question.text: Scenario: Arun Kumar is analyzing Reliance Industries (RIL) stock using multiple technical indicators. Current observations: (1) RIL CMP: Rs.2,850. (2) 50-day SMA: Rs.2,780 (rising). 200-day SMA: Rs.2,650 (rising). (3) The 50-day SMA crossed above the 200-day SMA one month ago. (4) RSI: 58. (5) Bollinger Bands: Upper band Rs.2,950, Middle band Rs.2,780, Lower band Rs.2,610. Price is between middle and upper band. (6) Strong support at Rs.2,600 (tested 3 times in last 6 months). (7) Resistance at Rs.2,950 (tested twice). (8) Volume has been above average on recent up-days. (9) MACD is above the signal line and above the zero baseline. Question 3 of 5: MACD above the signal line AND above zero baseline indicates:

answer.text: An established uptrend that is likely to continue, with positive momentum above the zero reference
A neutral market with no trend

explanation.text: **The Logic:** MACD above signal line = bullish momentum. MACD above zero baseline = confirmed bull market. Both together = strongly bullish. **The Trap:** The zero baseline distinguishes confirmed bull (above) from confirmed bear (below). Signal line cross shows momentum direction. **Cross-Exam Context:** Ch 15 NISM XV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** MACD above zero AND above signal line is the strongest bullish configuration. Only turn cautious when MACD starts flattening or diverging from price.
